# Provenance & reproducibility

What in this package is **reproducible from scratch** vs **shipped frozen**.

## Reproducible here

- **Scoring** (`score.py`) — deterministic. Same predictions in → same metrics out.
- **New-model prediction** (`run_classifier.py`) — deterministic (argmax, no
  sampling). Reproducible *given the same model weights and the pinned
  `transformers`/`torch` versions* in `requirements.txt`. Different library
  versions can shift ESM logits on borderline sequences.

## Shipped frozen (not regenerated by this package)

### Benchmark datasets (`*/data/benchmark.tsv`, `*/data/benchmark.fasta`)

- **inphared** — CDS proteins from INPHARED phage genomes deposited after the
  Aug-2025 training cutoff; product-name labelled, mmseqs2-clustered at 90%id,
  then deduplicated to <50% identity vs the RBPdetect2 training set.
- **experimental** — proteins with experimental evidence from UniProt
  (ECO:0000269), PDB (Caudoviricetes), and BRENDA (capsule depolymerase ECs),
  each assigned an evidence tier (T1 direct assay → T3 structure-only).

The build pipeline (GenBank fetch → parse/label → cluster → dedup → tier) lives
in the source repo under `benchmark/scripts/` and
`benchmark/benchmark_3_experimental/scripts/`; it is **not bundled** because it
needs NCBI access, mmseqs2, and hmmer/Pfam. The datasets here are the committed
outputs of that pipeline.

### Baseline tool predictions (`*/predictions/*.tsv`)

Produced once, with each tool at its **default settings**, then frozen. To
regenerate any of them you must install that tool and its database:

| Prediction file      | Tool / version                        | Notes |
|----------------------|---------------------------------------|-------|
| `rbpdetect2.tsv`     | RBPdetect2 (previous model, this work)| 3-class ESM-2 |
| `rbpdetect_v4.tsv`   | PhageRBPdetect v4 (Boeckaerts 2024)   | binary ESM |
| `phold.tsv`          | Phold (Bouras 2024)                   | ProstT5 3Di + Foldseek vs PHROG; `function==tail` = positive |
| `pharokka.tsv`       | Pharokka (Bouras 2023)                | MMseqs2 + pyhmmer; `category==tail` = positive |
| `phanns.tsv`         | PhANNs (Cantu 2020)                   | inphared only |
| `blastp.tsv`         | BLASTp vs training set                | experimental only; 3-class |
| `deposcope.tsv`      | DepoScope (Concha-Eloko 2024)         | depolymerase; scored TSP-only |
| `spikehunter.tsv`    | SpikeHunter (Yang 2024)               | tailspike; scored TSP-only |

These are frozen because the baselines do not change. Re-running them would
require the ~50 GB of third-party tools and databases that are intentionally
not part of this package. The new model is the only thing meant to be re-run.

## Bottom line

- Re-evaluating a new RBPdetect2 model: **fully reproducible** with the 2-step
  predict + score flow in the README.
- Rebuilding the datasets or baseline predictions from raw inputs: needs the
  external tools/DBs and the source-repo build scripts; out of scope here.
